description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crayfish, featuring a robust, segmented body with a hard exoskeleton in shades of brown and muted orange. The cephalothorax is broad and armored, with two large, prominent claws (chelae) extending forward, each textured with subtle ridges and color gradations. The abdomen is segmented and tapers toward the tail, with several pairs of articulated walking legs visible beneath the body. Two long, thin antennae project from the head, and small, dark eyes are positioned on short stalks. The surface appears rough and matte, with naturalistic color transitions and realistic anatomical proportions. This crustacean would be found in aquatic environments but could appear in urban settings as food or as a decorative or educational specimen.
description: A lifelike crayfish with a segmented brown-orange exoskeleton, large claws, and long antennae, featuring detailed legs and a rough, matte surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crayfish, both claws, segmented body, and all walking legs.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail fan, segmentation of the abdomen, and the symmetry of the legs and claws.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the crayfish, with claws and legs visible, and the tail slightly curled.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the large claws, antennae, and the placement of the eyes on short stalks.
